channel.title: NBC Sports Fantasy Football News
channel.description: Latest player news and analysis for fantasy football from NBC Sports.

item.title: Packers re-signed DL Brenton Cox Jr. to a one-year, $2.5 million contract.
item.description: Cox was scheduled to be a restricted free agent, so this will act as his last year of team control for the Packers. He’s played 15 games in three years for the Packers, and had all four of his career sacks in 2024.

item.title: Dolphins re-signed LB Willie Gay Jr. to a one-year contract.
item.description: The 2020 second-rounder started just two games for the Dolphins last year after coming over in free agency, but rebounded well from his disastrous 2024 season in New Orleans by posting a 66.7 PFF defensive grade. He’ll be in the mix at linebacker next year for the rebuilding Dolphins.

item.title: Browns agreed to terms with TE Jack Stoll, formerly of the Saints, on a one-year contract.
item.description: You better not wait on a blocking tight end in free agency, folks, the new meta is here. Stoll, 28, played 15 games for the Saints last year and was targeted 11 times in 209 snaps. He’s here to forge holes, not catch balls.

item.title: Dolphins agreed to terms with Josh Uche, formerly of the Eagles, on a one-year contract.
item.description: Since his 11.5-sack 2022, Uche has just six sacks in three seasons and his playing time has dwindled. He played 244 defensive snaps last year for the Eagles — a room that needed help on the edge desperately — and finished with just one sack. He’ll try to revive his career in Miami.

item.title: Falcons signed LB Channing Tindall, formerly of the Cardinals, to a one-year contract.
item.description: Tindall has played 25 defensive snaps in four NFL seasons since being picked late in the third round by the Dolphins. He’ll be a primary special-teamer for the Falcons in 2026.

item.title: Jets agreed to terms with Cade York, formerly of the Saints, on a one-year contract.
item.description: He’ll likely be in a kicking competition after Nick Folk fled the Jets for the Falcons this week. York didn’t attempt any field goals in the league last year after bouncing between two teams in 2024. The fourth-round pick of the Browns has hit just 33 of his first 45 NFL field-goal attempts.

item.title: Seahawks re-signed OL Josh Jones to a one-year contract.
item.description: Jones started three games and played in 14 for the Seahawks as their swing tackle in 2025. He returns for the same role in 2026. The soon-to-be 29-year-old posted a solid 73.1 PFF blocking grade in 261 snaps last season.

item.title: Colts agree to terms with EDGE Micheal Clemons, formerly of the Jets, on a three-year, $17.5 million contract.
item.description: Clemons can make another million dollars via incentives. The former Jet totaled 4.5 sacks in 2024 as a full-time starter. His role declined last year and he fell to just one sack. A three-year deal suggests the Colts plan to give him more reps in 2026.

item.title: Seahawks re-signed LS Chris Stoll to a two-year contract.
item.description: As the name implies, Stoll snaps the ball far. He has been doing so in Seattle for the past three seasons and will continue to do so over the next two seasons.

item.title: Jets re-signed OT Max Mitchell to a one-year contract.
item.description: Mitchell played just 73 snaps last year and hasn’t topped 500 snaps in a season yet since being drafted in the fourth round in 2022. He’ll return to a backup role on the Jets offensive line.
